items.title: The Intermediary: Waymo, the Whitney Biennial, and Fantasy of Automation
items.author: Shannon Kim
items.content:
<article>Hello !!! :) 
Thank you so much for 100k :,) the idea that people watch these in the first place is still baffling to me, nevertheless 100,000 people… wow

Actual video starts at 9:16 so skip ahead if you don't want to hear me oversharing

This video is on why silicon valley and the contemporary art world are failing in exactly the same way, my thoughts on the REACTIONS to the Whitney Biennial 2026, the role of the intellectual and what William Gaddis’s dying piano player in Agape Agape 

________________

REFERENCES:
Waymo defends use of remote assistance workers in US robotaxi operations - https://www.reuters.com/technology/waymo-defends-use-remote-assistance-workers-robotaxi-operations-2026-02-17/

Agapē Agape - WIlliam Gaddis

What Comes After Farce - Hal Foster

On the Mode of Existence of Technical Objects - Gilbert Simondon

Art and Emancipation in the Gaze of Political Eschatology - Keti Chukhrov

Road to Nowhere: What Silicon Valley Gets Wrong about the Future of Transportation - Paris Marx

The Future of the New: Artistic Innovation in Times of Social Acceleration - Thijs Lijster

The Guggenheim’s ‘Kiss of Death’: How a Career High Can Spell a Market Low 
https://news.artnet.com/market/ross-bleckner-guggenheim-retrospective-2628005

The Entrepreneur: The Classic Texts - Joseph A. Schumpeter</article>
